1|/**
     2| * IVX Senior Developer Narrative Gate.
     3| *
     4| * The IVX Owner AI chat (and the `/public/chat` fallback it uses) is a text
     5| * model. When the owner asks about patches, development, fixes, QA, deployment,
     6| * code, files changed, or "Senior Developer" work, the model has been observed
     7| * inventing fake patch summaries — e.g. "Workspace Inspection Results",
     8| * "Recent Patches", "Files Changed: src/investorDiscovery.js, src/dealManager.js",
     9| * "Deploy Authorization Needed", "If you need further details". None of that is
    10| * real Senior Developer proof: the chat model cannot read the repo, run tests,
    11| * commit, or deploy. Those are produced ONLY by the real Senior Developer proof
    12| * system (Admin → IVX Developer Workspace → Patches → Run Senior Developer),
    13| * which runs from the signed-in owner app session.
    14| *
    15| * This gate enforces the owner's required behavior:
    16| *  - When a prompt is about Senior Developer / patch / QA / deploy / code work,
    17| *    OR the model answer contains any fabricated developer-narrative marker, the
    18| *    answer is replaced.
    19| *  - If real Senior Developer proof is attached, the answer is the strict proof
    20| *    block (OWNER_AUTH_ACCEPTED / FILES_CHANGED / ... / FINAL_STATUS).
    21| *  - Otherwise the answer is the strict BLOCKED block (BLOCKED / REASON /
    22| *    EXACT_ACTION_REQUIRED).
    23| *
    24| * Pure + deterministic (no I/O, network, or AI) so it is fully unit-testable.
    25| */
    26|
    27|export const IVX_SENIOR_DEVELOPER_NARRATIVE_GATE_MARKER =
    28|  'ivx-senior-developer-narrative-gate-2026-06-15-extended';
    29|
    30|/**
    31| * Prompt keywords that route to Senior Developer proof mode. When the owner's
    32| * message mentions any of these, the chat model is NOT allowed to answer with a
    33| * free-text patch/dev narrative — it must return real proof or BLOCKED.
    34| */
    35|const SENIOR_DEVELOPER_PROMPT_PATTERNS: RegExp[] = [
    36|  /\bsenior developer\b/i,
    37|  /\brecent patches\b/i,
    38|  /\bpatches?\b/i,
    39|  /\bworkspace inspection\b/i,
    40|  /\bfiles? changed\b/i,
    41|  /\bwhat changed\b/i,
    42|  /\bdeploy(?:ed|ment|s)?\b/i,
    43|  /\bqa\b/i,
    44|  /\bquality assurance\b/i,
    45|  /\bfix(?:es|ed)?\b/i,
    46|  /\bbuild(?:s|ing)?\b/i,
    47|  /\blogs?\b/i,
    48|  /\bverification\b/i,
    49|  /\bverify\b/i,
    50|  /\bcommit(?:s|ted)?\b/i,
    51|  // Deployment / GitHub / Render / verification surface — every path the owner
    52|  // listed must route to real proof or BLOCKED, never a free-text narrative.
    53|  /\bgithub\b/i,
    54|  /\brender\b/i,
    55|  /\brollback\b/i,
    56|  /\bredeploy\b/i,
    57|  /\bpipeline\b/i,
    58|  /\bci\/?cd\b/i,
    59|  /\bpull request\b/i,
    60|  /\bmerged?\b/i,
    61|  /\brelease[ds]?\b/i,
    62|  /\bcommit_?sha\b/i,
    63|  /\blive_?commit\b/i,
    64|  /\bcommit_?match\b/i,
    65|  /\bdeploy_?id\b/i,
    66|  /\/version\b/i,
    67|  /\bproduction\b/i,
    68|];
    69|
    70|/**
    71| * Forbidden fabricated-narrative markers. If the model answer contains any of
    72| * these, it is inventing patch summaries / fake files / fake authorization and
    73| * the answer is blocked. Sourced from the exact bad response the owner reported.
    74| */
    75|const FORBIDDEN_NARRATIVE_MARKERS: { marker: RegExp; label: string }[] = [
    76|  { marker: /workspace inspection results/i, label: 'Workspace Inspection Results' },
    77|  { marker: /recent patches/i, label: 'Recent Patches' },
    78|  { marker: /investor discovery/i, label: 'Investor Discovery' },
    79|  { marker: /deal management/i, label: 'Deal Management' },
    80|  { marker: /src\/investorDiscovery\.js/i, label: 'src/investorDiscovery.js' },
    81|  { marker: /src\/dealManager\.js/i, label: 'src/dealManager.js' },
    82|  { marker: /deploy authorization needed/i, label: 'Deploy Authorization Needed' },
    83|  { marker: /if you (?:want to proceed|need further details)/i, label: 'If you need further details' },
    84|  // Fabricated first-person dev/deploy claims — the chat model can never have
    85|  // actually committed, pushed, deployed, or verified, so any such claim is fake.
    86|  { marker: /\bI(?:'ve| have)? (?:just )?(?:committed|deployed|pushed|merged|rolled back)\b/i, label: 'fabricated first-person dev action' },
    87|  { marker: /successfully (?:deployed|committed|pushed|merged|built|tested)/i, label: 'fabricated success claim' },
    88|  { marker: /deployment (?:complete|completed|successful|succeeded|is live)/i, label: 'fabricated deployment status' },
    89|  { marker: /(?:pushed|committed) to (?:github|main|production|the repo)/i, label: 'fabricated push/commit claim' },
    90|  { marker: /render deploy(?:ment)? (?:triggered|started|complete|succeeded|is live)/i, label: 'fabricated Render deploy claim' },
    91|  { marker: /(?:tests?|typecheck|build) (?:passed|succeeded|are green)/i, label: 'fabricated test/build result' },
    92|  { marker: /all (?:checks|tests) pass(?:ed|ing)?/i, label: 'fabricated all-checks-pass claim' },
    93|  // Fabricated "STATUS: DEPLOYED" / "STATUS: COMPLETED" claims when real
    94|  // execution fields are absent — the model invents a top-line status that
    95|  // contradicts the per-field "NOT VERIFIED" / "not run" evidence.
    96|  { marker: /^STATUS:\s*(?:DEPLOYED|COMPLETED|SUCCESS|DONE)\s*$/im, label: 'fabricated STATUS: DEPLOYED/COMPLETED claim' },
    97|  { marker: /(?<!UN)VERIFIED/i, label: 'fabricated VERIFIED claim (not preceded by UN)' },
    98|];
    99|
   100|/**
   101| * Whether a prompt should be routed to Senior Developer proof mode.
   102| * Pure — deterministic.
   103| */
   104|export function isSeniorDeveloperProofPrompt(message: string): boolean {
   105|  const text = typeof message === 'string' ? message : '';
   106|  if (text.trim().length === 0) return false;
   107|  return SENIOR_DEVELOPER_PROMPT_PATTERNS.some((pattern) => pattern.test(text));
   108|}
   109|
   110|/**
   111| * The fabricated-narrative markers present in an answer.
   112| * Pure — deterministic.
   113| */
   114|export function findForbiddenNarrativeMarkers(answer: string): string[] {
   115|  const text = typeof answer === 'string' ? answer : '';
   116|  return FORBIDDEN_NARRATIVE_MARKERS.filter(({ marker }) => marker.test(text)).map(({ label }) => label);
   117|}
   118|
   119|/**
   120| * Real Senior Developer proof — produced ONLY by the live owner-session run
   121| * (Admin → IVX Developer Workspace → Patches → Run Senior Developer). The chat
   122| * model never has this; it is attached by the caller when a real run completed.
   123| */
   124|export type SeniorDeveloperProof = {
   125|  ownerAuthAccepted: boolean;
   126|  filesChanged: string[];
   127|  rawTestOutput: string | null;
   128|  rawTypecheckOutput: string | null;
   129|  commitSha: string | null;
   130|  renderDeployId: string | null;
   131|  liveCommit: string | null;
   132|  commitMatch: boolean;
   133|  finalStatus: string;
   134|};
   135|
   136|/**
   137| * The strict proof block returned when real Senior Developer proof exists.
   138| * Never contains free-text narrative.
   139| */
   140|export function buildSeniorDeveloperProofMessage(proof: SeniorDeveloperProof): string {
   141|  const filesChanged = proof.filesChanged.length > 0 ? proof.filesChanged.join(', ') : 'none';
   142|  return [
   143|    `OWNER_AUTH_ACCEPTED=${proof.ownerAuthAccepted ? 'true' : 'false'}`,
   144|    `FILES_CHANGED=${filesChanged}`,
   145|    `RAW_TEST_OUTPUT=${proof.rawTestOutput ?? 'none'}`,
   146|    `RAW_TYPECHECK_OUTPUT=${proof.rawTypecheckOutput ?? 'none'}`,
   147|    `COMMIT_SHA=${proof.commitSha ?? 'none'}`,
   148|    `RENDER_DEPLOY_ID=${proof.renderDeployId ?? 'none'}`,
   149|    `LIVE_COMMIT=${proof.liveCommit ?? 'none'}`,
   150|    `COMMIT_MATCH=${proof.commitMatch ? 'true' : 'false'}`,
   151|    `FINAL_STATUS=${proof.finalStatus}`,
   152|  ].join('\n');
   153|}
   154|
   155|/**
   156| * The strict BLOCKED block returned when no real proof exists. This is the only
   157| * honest answer the chat model can give for Senior Developer work — it cannot
   158| * run the proof system itself.
   159| */
   160|export function buildSeniorDeveloperBlockedMessage(reason?: string): string {
   161|  return [
   162|    'BLOCKED',
   163|    `REASON=${
   164|      reason ??
   165|      'The IVX Owner AI chat cannot read the repository, run tests, commit, or deploy. Real Senior Developer proof is produced only by the live run system, not by this chat reply. No verified proof is attached to this turn.'
   166|    }`,
   167|    'EXACT_ACTION_REQUIRED=Open the IVX app signed in as the owner → Admin → IVX Developer Workspace → Patches → Run Senior Developer. The on-screen proof (OWNER_AUTH_ACCEPTED, FILES_CHANGED, COMMIT_SHA, RENDER_DEPLOY_ID, LIVE_COMMIT, COMMIT_MATCH, FINAL_STATUS) is the only valid Senior Developer proof.',
   168|  ].join('\n');
   169|}
   170|
   171|export type SeniorDeveloperNarrativeGateInput = {
   172|  /** The owner's prompt for this turn. */
   173|  message: string;
   174|  /** The model's drafted answer. */
   175|  answer: string;
   176|  /** Real proof from a completed live run, when one is attached. */
   177|  proof?: SeniorDeveloperProof | null;
   178|};
   179|
   180|export type SeniorDeveloperNarrativeGateResult = {
   181|  /** The answer to return (rewritten when the gate intervenes). */
   182|  answer: string;
   183|  /** Whether the gate replaced the model answer. */
   184|  gated: boolean;
   185|  /** Whether the routed prompt was a Senior Developer proof prompt. */
   186|  routed: boolean;
   187|  /** Fabricated-narrative markers detected in the model answer. */
   188|  forbiddenMarkers: string[];
   189|};
   190|
   191|/**
   192| * Apply the Senior Developer Narrative Gate to a chat answer.
   193| *
   194| * The model answer is replaced when EITHER the prompt is a Senior Developer
   195| * proof prompt OR the answer contains a fabricated-narrative marker. In both
   196| * cases the replacement is real proof (when attached) or the strict BLOCKED
   197| * block. Otherwise the answer passes through unchanged.
   198| *
   199| * Pure — deterministic, no I/O.
   200| */
   201|export function applySeniorDeveloperNarrativeGate(
   202|  input: SeniorDeveloperNarrativeGateInput,
   203|): SeniorDeveloperNarrativeGateResult {
   204|  const routed = isSeniorDeveloperProofPrompt(input.message);
   205|  const forbiddenMarkers = findForbiddenNarrativeMarkers(input.answer);
   206|
   207|  if (!routed && forbiddenMarkers.length === 0) {
   208|    return { answer: input.answer, gated: false, routed, forbiddenMarkers };
   209|  }
   210|
   211|  const proof = input.proof ?? null;
   212|  const answer =
   213|    proof && proof.ownerAuthAccepted
   214|      ? buildSeniorDeveloperProofMessage(proof)
   215|      : buildSeniorDeveloperBlockedMessage(
   216|          forbiddenMarkers.length > 0
   217|            ? 'The chat model attempted to fabricate a Senior Developer / patch narrative. That is never real proof, and the chat cannot read the repo, run tests, commit, or deploy. No verified live-run proof is attached to this turn.'
   218|            : undefined,
   219|        );
   220|
   221|  return { answer, gated: true, routed, forbiddenMarkers };
   222|}
